prerequisites: A TaskRouter Workspace, which is the top-level container holding all TaskRouter resources (Workers, Activities, Task Queues, Workflows, Tasks, and statistics).
Your account_sid: the unique Twilio Account SID, found in the Twilio Console under the Account Dashboard.
Your auth_token: the Twilio Auth Token paired with that Account SID, found in the Twilio Console under the Account Dashboard.

authMethods.name: Account SID and Auth Token (HTTP Basic Auth)
authMethods.summary: Twilio's REST API, including the TaskRouter API at https://taskrouter.twilio.com, authenticates with HTTP Basic authentication. Provide your Account SID (account_sid) as the username and your Auth Token (auth_token) as the password. Both values come from the Twilio Console Account Dashboard. The connector sends these credentials over HTTPS on every request.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Twilio credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Twilio Console and open the Account Dashboard. Copy your Account SID and Auth Token from the account info panel. These are the two values the connector requires.

setupSteps.title: Confirm a TaskRouter Workspace exists
setupSteps.body: Ensure your Twilio account has at least one TaskRouter Workspace. The Workspace is the container for all TaskRouter objects (Workers, Activities, Task Channels, Task Queues, Workflows, Tasks, Statistics, and Events) that the API exposes under https://taskrouter.twilio.com.

setupSteps.title: Enter the account_sid field
setupSteps.body: Paste your Twilio Account SID into the account_sid field. It is used as the username for HTTP Basic authentication against the Twilio TaskRouter API.

setupSteps.title: Enter the auth_token field
setupSteps.body: Paste your Twilio Auth Token into the auth_token field. It is used as the password for HTTP Basic authentication. Keep this value secret and rotate it from the Twilio Console if it is ever exposed.

sources.title: Making requests to the Twilio API (HTTP Basic authentication with Account SID and Auth Token)
